column5:
This experience is *inspired by TV game shows and escape rooms*.

It* *combines *thrills* with physical and mental *challenges*.

You go to a *purpose-built venue* with *2 to 15 other people*, 
and you all play *a series of minigames*.

column28: I love the idea.  There’s something like this called Play in Vegas that looked so fun when I was there recently.  But as someone who spends a lot of time alone and doesn’t have a lot of people to bring to stuff like this, it’d be nice if there would be a way to be added to a team that isn’t full or have singles event times where you buy a ticket and are put into a group rather than having to come with others to get the full value out of it.

column28: Your immersive experience for Machu Pichu was awesome. While the processing power does not allow for crystal clear video, the effect was spectacular and very enjoyable. Your price for admission to the Machu Pichu experience was very reasonable. We have seen the Van Gogh exhibit and loved that as well. Since we are older, taking us on art/travel experiences is awesome; please continue to do that. My other suggestion is to use popular stories and build an experience out of that. I believe you do something with Harry Potter which appeals to the younger crowd and us (we are 65 and 73). You could also do something with Greek mythology, life in Rome, a trip through national parks (Yellowstone, Mesa Verde, Zion, Bryce, ...), and travel through time in one general location to see geographic changes. I suspect you have already thought of these ideas and want to encourage you to continue considering them. If processing power allows, it might be advantageous to run different adventures at the same location. We will be looking for more options in the future.
For Candlelight concerts please consider doing Ariana Grande or BTS please
